perturbation.original_text: The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records in accordance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to, records required by Section 31(a) of the Act and the rules thereunder, as the same may be amended from time to time, pertaining to the various functions performed by it and not otherwise created and maintained by another party pursuant to contract with the Trust. Where applicable, such records shall be maintained by the Adviser for the periods and in the places required by Rule 31a-2 under the Act.
perturbation.changed_text: The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records in accordance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to, records required by Section 31(a) of the Act and the rules thereunder, as the same may be amended from time to time, pertaining to the various functions performed by it and not otherwise created and maintained by another party pursuant to contract with the Trust. Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary, all records pertaining to fund performance calculations need only be retained for a period of one year. Where applicable, such records shall be maintained by the Adviser for the periods and in the places required by Rule 31a-2 under the Act.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding record keeping. Section 4 initially states that the Adviser will maintain all records as required by Section 31(a) of the Act and Rule 31a-2. However, the added sentence creates an exception, stating that fund performance records need only be kept for one year. This contradicts the initial, broader statement and creates uncertainty in enforcement, particularly if Rule 31a-2 requires a longer retention period for such records. This creates an in-text contradiction within the same section of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Record Keeping and Other Information. The Adviser shall create and maintain all necessary records in accordance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, including, but not limited to, records required by Section 31(a) of the Act and the rules thereunder, as the same may be amended from time to time, pertaining to the various functions performed by it and not otherwise created and maintained by another party pursuant to contract with the Trust. Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary, all records pertaining to fund performance calculations need only be retained for a period of one year. Where applicable, such records shall be maintained by the Adviser for the periods and in the places required by Rule 31a-2 under the Act.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Adviser shall maintain records according to Section 31(a) of the Act and Rule 31a-2, implying a standard retention period. The changed text adds a sentence stating that fund performance records only need to be kept for one year, creating a specific exception. This directly contradicts the earlier broad statement about adhering to Rule 31a-2 and creates an ambiguity as to whether Rule 31a-2 might require a longer retention period for fund performance records, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This falls under the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Adviser may rely on information reasonably believed by it to be accurate and reliable. Except as may otherwise be required by the Act or the rules thereunder, neither the Adviser nor its directors, officers, employees, shareholders, agents, control persons or affiliates of any thereof (collectively, the "Adviser Employees") shall be subject to any liability for, or any damages, expenses or losses incurred by the Trust in connection with any error of judgment, mistake of law, any act or omission in connection with or arising out of any services rendered under or payments made pursuant to this Agreement or any other matter to which this Agreement relates, except by reason of willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence on the part of any such persons in the performance of the duties of the Adviser under this Agreement or by reason of reckless disregard by any of such persons of the obligations and duties of the Adviser under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Adviser may rely on information reasonably believed by it to be accurate and reliable. Notwithstanding the above, the Adviser is ultimately responsible for verifying the accuracy of all data used in fund management. Except as may otherwise be required by the Act or the rules thereunder, neither the Adviser nor its directors, officers, employees, shareholders, agents, control persons or affiliates of any thereof (collectively, the "Adviser Employees") shall be subject to any liability for, or any damages, expenses or losses incurred by the Trust in connection with any error of judgment, mistake of law, any act or omission in connection with or arising out of any services rendered under or payments made pursuant to this Agreement or any other matter to which this Agreement relates, except by reason of willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence on the part of any such persons in the performance of the duties of the Adviser under this Agreement or by reason of reckless disregard by any of such persons of the obligations and duties of the Adviser under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 7 regarding the Adviser's liability. While the original text allows the Adviser to rely on reasonably believed accurate information and limits their liability to instances of willful misconduct, the added sentence asserts the Adviser is 'ultimately responsible for verifying the accuracy of all data used in fund management'. The original limitation of liability creates an in-text contradiction of terms in this section, since the Adviser cannot be held ultimately responsible if they are relying on data from a third party that they reasonably believed was accurate, and makes enforcement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Limitation of Liability. The Adviser may rely on information reasonably believed by it to be accurate and reliable. Notwithstanding the above, the Adviser is ultimately responsible for verifying the accuracy of all data used in fund management. Except as may otherwise be required by the Act or the rules thereunder, neither the Adviser nor its directors, officers, employees, shareholders, agents, control persons or affiliates of any thereof (collectively, the "Adviser Employees") shall be subject to any liability for, or any damages, expenses or losses incurred by the Trust in connection with any error of judgment, mistake of law, any act or omission in connection with or arising out of any services rendered under or payments made pursuant to this Agreement or any other matter to which this Agreement relates, except by reason of willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence on the part of any such persons in the performance of the duties of the Adviser under this Agreement or by reason of reckless disregard by any of such persons of the obligations and duties of the Adviser under this Agreement. Any person, even though also a director, officer, employee, shareholder or agent of the Adviser, who may be or become a trustee, officer, employee or agent of the Trust, shall be deemed, when rendering services to the Trust or 3 acting on any business of the Trust (other than services or business in connection with the Adviser's duties hereunder), to be rendering such services to or acting solely for the Trust and not as a director, officer, employee, shareholder or agent, or one under the control or direction of the Adviser, even though paid by it. The Adviser is responsible for any information that it provides to the Trust for inclusion in the prospectus and/or statement of additional information of the Fund. The Adviser agrees to defend and indemnify the Trust, and its trustees, officers and agent from and against any and all liability, losses, claims and damages arising from any inaccurate or incomplete statement provided by the Adviser (or its officers, employees, directors and agents) for inclusion in the Fund's prospectus or statement of additional information. The Adviser agrees to defend and indemnify the Trust, and its trustees, officers and agents from and against any and all liability, losses, claims and damages arising from your willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence on the Adviser's part or the part of the Adviser's officers, employees, directors and agents in the performance of the Adviser's duties under this Agreement, or by reason of reckless disregard by any of such persons of the Adviser's obligations and duties under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text allows the Adviser to rely on information reasonably believed to be accurate, limiting liability. The added sentence, 'Notwithstanding the above, the Adviser is ultimately responsible for verifying the accuracy of all data used in fund management,' directly conflicts with the original liability limitation. The Adviser cannot 'rely' on information and simultaneously be 'ultimately responsible' for its accuracy. This creates an ambiguity and unenforceability, leading to disputes over contractual obligations and is classified as Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction as there are inconsistent definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended at any time by written instrument executed by each of the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended at any time by written instrument executed by each of the parties hereto. However, changes to the compensation structure outlined in Section 6 require unanimous consent from the Board of Trustees of the Trust.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds a condition to amending the agreement. Section 11 initially states the agreement can be amended by written instrument executed by both parties. However, the added sentence introduces a specific requirement for unanimous consent from the Board of Trustees for changes to Section 6 (compensation). This directly contradicts the initial statement that any amendment only requires written agreement from both parties. The term "amended" is now contradicted. The contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement since it's unclear which process takes precedence. The change creates an in-text contradiction in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. Each party agrees to perform such further acts and execute such further documents as are necessary to effectuate the purposes hereof. This Agreement shall be construed and enforced in accordance with and governed by the laws of the State of Massachusetts. This Agreement may be amended at any time by written instrument executed by each of the parties hereto. However, changes to the compensation structure outlined in Section 6 require unanimous consent from the Board of Trustees of the Trust. The captions in this Agreement are included for convenience of reference only and in no way define or delimit any of the provisions hereof or otherwise affect their construction or effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can be amended by written instrument executed by each of the parties. The changed text adds a requirement for unanimous consent from the Board of Trustees for changes to Section 6. This directly contradicts the original statement because it introduces an additional condition for amendment in specific circumstances, creating an in-text contradiction in this section. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
